steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for a difference in how the two authors view the benefits of the City of Gastronomy designation. Text 1 says "Simply by selecting Mérida, UNESCO has helped bring awareness ... and has provided a significant boost" — benefits flow automatically from selection. Text 2 counters with "However, cities must still create effective marketing strategies to benefit fully" — benefits require intentional effort.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The author of Text 1 considers the effects of Mérida's City of Gastronomy status to be less permanent than the author of Text 2 considers them to be': Neither text makes any claim about how permanent the benefits are. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The author of Text 1 states that the benefits are automatic, while the author of Text 2 states that effort is required to fully achieve the benefits': Text 1 uses "simply by selecting" to frame benefits as automatic; Text 2 uses "must still create effective marketing strategies" to frame them as requiring deliberate work. ✓
  • Choice C — 'The author of Text 1 views Mérida's status as having been beneficial for the city, but the author of Text 2 questions whether there's been a benefit': Text 2 does not question whether benefits exist — it argues that full benefits require effort. ✗
  • Choice D — 'The author of Text 1 assumes restaurant owners and chefs are most likely to benefit, but the author of Text 2 states the honor most affects tour guides and tour operators': Tour guides and tour operators appear nowhere in either text. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
